name: N.e.w.
description_short: N.E.W. Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit financial cooperative serving Wisconsin and Michigan communities since 1962, offering loans, savings accounts, and financial education.
description_long:
N.E.W. Credit Union has operated as a member-owned financial cooperative since 1962, serving Brown, Shawano, Oconto, and Marinette counties in Wisconsin, as well as Menominee County, Michigan. As a not-for-profit institution insured by the NCUA, the credit union is structured to prioritize member benefit over shareholder profit, allowing it to reinvest surplus revenue back into competitive rates and community programs.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of consumer financial products including consumer loans (as low as 4.25% APR), mortgages (as low as 4.99% APR), personal loans (as low as 9.50% APR), and share savings accounts (starting at 0.15% APY). Members also have access to digital banking services including mobile deposits, bill payment, fund transfers, and account monitoring through online and mobile platforms. The institution provides financial empowerment resources through an online education center covering digital banking security and financial improvement strategies, along with personalized financial counseling services.

N.E.W. Credit Union distinguishes itself through strong community involvement and member loyalty programs. The organization actively supports local organizations and events, particularly in Oconto County, and has implemented a "once a member, always a member" policy allowing members who relocate outside the service area to retain membership benefits indefinitely. The credit union participates in the shared branching network, enabling members to access services nationwide despite having limited physical branch locations.

The credit union's primary limitations stem from its geographic field of membership, which restricts eligibility to residents or employees of specific Wisconsin and Michigan counties and their families. While competitive rates are advertised, the actual APR and APY offerings require individual qualification and may vary based on creditworthiness. The online-only education center and digital banking options may not fully substitute for in-person financial counseling availability across all service areas.
services: Consumer loans with rates as low as 4.25% APR
Mortgage lending with rates starting at 4.99% APR
Personal loans with rates from 9.50% APR
Share savings accounts starting at 0.15% APY
Mobile and online banking with account balance checking and transaction review
Bill payment and fund transfer services between member accounts
Online education center for digital banking security and financial improvement
Community sponsorship and volunteer partnership opportunities
pros: Competitive consumer loan rates starting at 4.25% APR and mortgage rates from 4.99% APR
Member-owned structure ensures profits are reinvested into member benefits rather than distributed to external shareholders
"Once a member, always a member" policy allows members to retain membership and benefits even after relocating outside service areas
Free financial counseling services and online education center covering digital banking security and financial empowerment
Access to shared branching network enabling nationwide service access despite limited physical locations
Established 60+ year history serving local Wisconsin and Michigan communities with demonstrated community investment
NCUA insurance protection for member deposits up to standard limits
cons: Membership eligibility restricted to Brown, Shawano, Oconto, and Marinette counties in Wisconsin plus Menominee County, Michigan—excludes most U.S. residents
Limited information on website regarding specific branch locations, hours, and current member-exclusive rates without contacting the institution
Share savings account rate of 0.15% APY is relatively low compared to high-yield online savings alternatives
No clear details on loan approval timeline, credit score requirements, or specific underwriting criteria
Geographic limitation may reduce competitive pressure and service innovation compared to larger national credit unions
best_for: Residents and employees of Brown, Shawano, Oconto, or Marinette counties in Wisconsin seeking member-owned credit union membership
Borrowers in the service area looking for competitive consumer and mortgage loans with local community reinvestment benefits
Members seeking financial literacy resources and counseling support combined with digital banking capabilities
Individuals relocating outside the service area who want to maintain long-term credit union membership and history
diagnosis: N.E.W. Credit Union is best for individuals and families living or working in specific Wisconsin (Brown, Shawano, Oconto, Marinette) or Michigan (Menominee) counties who value member-owned cooperative banking with competitive rates and community involvement. The primary caveat is strict geographic membership eligibility that excludes most U.S. consumers, making it inaccessible unless you reside or work in the designated service area.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.

meta_description: N.e.w. — NCUA-insured credit union in Oconto Falls, WI. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
